factors.summary: The company has not experienced any material changes in risk factors since the previous filing.

evidence.text: There have been no material changes from the risk factors previously disclosed in our Form 10-K.
evidence.subsection: Item 1A. Risk Factors

factors.summary: The company is investing in its workforce, as indicated by increased compensation costs.

evidence.text: Selling, general and administrative expenses for the three months ended June 30, 2017 increased $46.6 million , or 10.7% , from the comparable period, reflecting the impact of acquisitions in 2016, including the acquisition-related depreciation and amortization expense, as well as increased compensation costs, partially offset by lower restructuring charges of $3.2 million .
evidence.subsection: Selling, General and Administrative Expenses

factors.summary: Overall demand appears to be accelerating, particularly in certain end markets.

evidence.text: Fluids segment revenue increased $273.6 million, or 34.0%, as compared to the six months ended June 30, 2016, attributable to 34.8% acquisition-related growth and organic growth of 0.7% partially offset by an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation of 1.5%.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2017 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2016

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, particularly in certain segments.

evidence.text: Backlog increased 20% over the prior year after adjusting for the disposition, with strong increases in both platforms.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2017 Compared to the Second Quarter 2016

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly steel, are rising, but the company is offsetting them through pricing and productivity.

evidence.text: Segment margin increased from 14.7% to 15.4% as pricing and productivity initiatives more than offset rising material costs, most notably steel.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2017 Compared to the Second Quarter 2016

factors.summary: Restructuring charges have been reduced, contributing to margin improvements.

evidence.text: Segment earnings increased $53.4 million, as compared to the prior year quarter, primarily driven by higher volume across our business and a reduction in restructuring expenses of $5.6 million compared to the prior year quarter.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2017 Compared to the Second Quarter 2016

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving, driven by productivity gains.

evidence.text: Benefits from productivity being offset by the impact of acquisitions. Sequentially, margins improved 330 basis points due to the benefits of integration.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2017 Compared to the Second Quarter 2016

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: We expect full year 2017 capital expenditures to approximate 2.4% of revenue.
evidence.subsection: Investing Activities

factors.summary: The company is deleveraging, with a net debt to net capitalization ratio improving.

evidence.text: Our net debt to net capitalization ratio decreased to 44.2% at June 30, 2017 from 46.3% at December 31, 2016.
evidence.subsection: Capitalization

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its dividend payments, showing confidence in its financial position.

evidence.text: Dividends paid to shareholders totaled $137.2 million as compared to $131.3 million during the same period in 2016.
evidence.subsection: Financing Activities

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, though lower than prior periods.

evidence.text: Free cash flow for the six months ended June 30, 2017 decreased $82.8 million compared to the prior year period.
evidence.subsection: Free Cash Flow

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its market share, with strong performance in key segments.

evidence.text: Our Refrigeration & Food Equipment segment is a provider of innovative and energy efficient equipment and systems serving the commercial refrigeration and food equipment end markets.
evidence.subsection: Refrigeration & Food Equipment

factors.summary: The competitive environment is manageable, with the company holding a strong position in key markets.

evidence.text: Our Energy segment, serving the Drilling & Production, Bearings & Compression and Automation end markets, is a provider of customer-driven solutions and services for safe and efficient production and processing of fuels worldwide and has a strong presence in the bearings and compression components and automation markets.
evidence.subsection: Energy

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include technology and market presence, which are strengthening.

evidence.text: The increase is driven by significant growth in U.S. rig count, increases in well completions and strong results in the Bearings & Compression end market.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2017 Compared to the Second Quarter 2016

factors.summary: Customer diversification is improving, reducing dependence on a few large customers.

evidence.text: Backlog increased 20% over the prior year after adjusting for the disposition, with strong increases in both platforms.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2017 Compared to the Second Quarter 2016

factors.summary: Economic conditions are favorable, acting as a tailwind with expansion and strong GDP growth.

evidence.text: Overall bookings increased 34.1% as compared to the prior year quarter, primarily driven by the aforementioned acquisitions. Book to bill was 1.00.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2017 Compared to the Second Quarter 2016

factors.summary: Interest rate changes are not significantly impacting the company, with manageable debt service costs.

evidence.text: We have not drawn down any loans under the facility nor do we anticipate doing so. Under the Credit Agreement, we are required to pay a facility fee and to maintain an interest coverage ratio of consolidated EBITDA to consolidated net interest expense of not less than 3.0 to 1.0.
evidence.subsection: Capitalization

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are creating some headwinds, particularly in revenue growth.

evidence.text: Partially offset by an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation of 1.5%.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2017 Compared to the Second Quarter 2016

evidence.text: No specific mention of geopolitical risks impacting the company's operations.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are building, partially offset by increases in accounts payable.

factors.summary: The factor volume_trends is positive as both subsections indicate growth. The Gross Profit subsection highlights increased sales volumes contributing to higher gross profit and revenue, while the Second Quarter 2017 Compared to the Second Quarter 2016 subsection points to unit volume growth driven by real demand rather than price increases.

evidence.text: Gross profit for the three months ended June 30, 2017 increased $118.2 million , or 18.7% , from the comparable period, reflecting benefits of increased sales volumes.
evidence.subsection: Gross Profit

evidence.text: Volume growth, including acquisitions and productivity gains, was a key driver of earnings increase.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2017 Compared to the Second Quarter 2016

factors.summary: The company has mixed forward guidance. While management has raised its revenue growth and earnings per share expectations, there is no specific forward guidance provided in the text.

evidence.text: There is no specific forward guidance provided in the text.
evidence.subsection: Item 1A. Risk Factors

evidence.text: We now expect full year revenue to increase approximately 12% to 14%. This forecast includes expected organic revenue growth of 5% to 7%. In total, full year diluted earnings per share is expected to be in the range of $4.23 to $4.33.
evidence.subsection: Revenue

evidence.text: We are targeting to generate free cash flow of approximately 11% of revenue for the full year 2017.
evidence.subsection: Free Cash Flow

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is showing mixed trends, with a notable improvement in the second quarter of 2017 compared to prior periods, but a slight deceleration over the six-month period when compared to previous years. The growth was broad-based across segments but faced challenges in transport markets, though it was supported by strong performance in industrial activity and key sectors like hygienic and pharma.

evidence.text: Results were driven by organic revenue growth of 10.0% , which was broad-based across our segments, and acquisition-related revenue growth of 11.7% primarily within our Fluids and Engineered Systems segments.
evidence.subsection: Revenue

evidence.text: Organic growth was driven by industrial activity and solid hygienic and pharma markets partially offset by continued weakness in transport markets.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2017 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2016

factors.summary: The end market momentum is mixed, with the Energy segment showing strong growth driven by U.S. rig count growth and increased well completion activity, while other end markets like retail refrigeration also experience growth. However, the transport and commercial cooking equipment markets are slowing, indicating a mixed momentum across different sectors.

evidence.text: The growth in organic revenue was led by our Energy segment, in which growth of 39.3% was driven by U.S rig count growth and increased well completion activity, as well as strong results in our Bearings & Compression end market.
evidence.subsection: Revenue

evidence.text: Refrigeration revenue increased 6.1% driven by market participation gains and key customer growth programs in retail refrigeration.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2017 Compared to the Second Quarter 2016

evidence.text: End markets such as retail refrigeration and oil and gas are growing fastest, while transport and commercial cooking equipment markets are slowing.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2017 Compared to the Second Quarter 2016

factors.summary: The company is experiencing positive margin trajectory with improvements in both gross and operating margins. This improvement is driven by pricing and productivity gains, which have outpaced rising material costs, particularly in steel.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin improved by 20 basis points for the three months ended June 30, 2017 from the comparable period.
evidence.subsection: Gross Profit

evidence.text: Segment margin increased from 14.7% to 15.4% as pricing and productivity initiatives more than offset rising material costs, most notably steel.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2017 Compared to the Second Quarter 2016

factors.summary: The company's pricing strategy shows mixed results. While customer pricing had a favorable impact on revenue in the second quarter of 2017, it did not significantly affect revenue when compared to the previous year's second quarter.

evidence.text: Customer pricing favorably impacted revenue by approximately 0.7% in the second quarter of 2017 .
evidence.subsection: Revenue

evidence.text: Customer pricing did not have a significant impact to revenue in the second quarter of 2017.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2017 Compared to the Second Quarter 2016

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, with notable growth in both the Energy and Refrigeration & Food Equipment sectors. The Energy segment experienced a significant increase of 39.3% driven by U.S. rig count growth and increased well completion activity, while the Refrigeration segment saw a 6.1% rise primarily due to market participation gains and customer growth programs.

evidence.text: The growth in organic revenue was led by our Energy segment, in which growth of 39.3% was driven by U.S rig count growth and increased well completion activity, as well as strong results in our Bearings & Compression end market.
evidence.subsection: Revenue

evidence.text: Refrigeration revenue increased $20.4 million, or 6.1%, as compared to the prior year quarter, primarily driven by market participation gains and key customer growth programs in retail refrigeration.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2017 Compared to the Second Quarter 2016

factors.summary: The company has been actively engaging in acquisitions, such as the purchase of Caldera Graphics S.A.S., to strengthen its market position and drive growth. The integration of Caldera has been successful, contributing to the company's expansion in the global digital textile printing market.

evidence.text: During the second quarter of 2017, we completed the acquisition of Caldera Graphics S.A.S. (''Caldera'') for approximately $32.7 million , net of cash acquired and including contingent consideration. Caldera enhances our ability to serve the global digital textile printing market with their high-quality technical software designed for the digital printing industry.
evidence.subsection: Revenue

evidence.text: During the six months ended June 30, 2017, we acquired Caldera within the Engineered Systems segment for a cash consideration of $25.6 million.
evidence.subsection: Investing Activities

factors.summary: Construction activity and infrastructure spending are positively impacting the Refrigeration & Food Equipment segment, contributing to growth and increased demand for the company's products.

evidence.text: Organic growth in our Refrigeration & Food Equipment segment increased 5.1%, driven by strong activity in the retail refrigeration market.
evidence.subsection: Revenue

evidence.text: Refrigeration revenue increased $20.4 million, or 6.1%, as compared to the prior year quarter, primarily driven by market participation gains and key customer growth programs in retail refrigeration.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter 2017 Compared to the Second Quarter 2016
